cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage again to understand what it's about. The author received a message from her former English teacher, Margaret Leibfried, expressing pride in her accomplishments. The author reflects on her high school days when she was an introverted student, the only non-white student in a school full of tall white athletes. She felt shy and often teased. Ms. Leibfried was her English teacher and noticed the author's interest in reading and writing. She offered her reading suggestions and even suggested a different approach for a book report. Instead of a standard report, the author could recite a passage from "The Bell Jar." The author was nervous but was applauded by the class, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for choosing a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. This experience made the author feel confident for the first time. Now, the question is: Why did Ms. Leibfried ask the author to recite a passage in front of the class? The choices are: (A) To test whether she had a good memory. (B) To make her earn the respect of her classmates. (C) To help deepen her understanding of "The Bell Jar." (D) To guide her to build a good relationship with others. Let's analyze the passage for clues. The author says that Ms. Leibfried "seemed to notice my interest in both reading and writing, and she took the time to make me feel less shy; she even offered me reading suggestions..."
